description: A celebrated collection of sayings attributed to the Buddha, emphasizing ethics, mindfulness, wisdom, and the path to liberation.
James Legge's English translation of the Tao Te Ching, a foundational Daoist text on the Way, virtue, simplicity, and non-action.
An English rendering of ancient Egyptian funerary texts intended to guide the deceased through judgment and the afterlife.
Ancient Indian philosophical and religious texts that explore Brahman, Atman, liberation, and the foundations of Vedantic thought.
English translations of devotional Japanese Buddhist poems associated with Shinran Shonin and Pure Land religious thought.
The foundational text of Confucianism, a record of the sayings of Confucius and his conversations with his disciples.

id: The Babylonian Legends of the Creation
description: A translation and discussion of Babylonian creation mythology, including themes of cosmic order, divine conflict, and the origins of the world.
A broad collection of Chinese myths, legends, deities, culture heroes, creation stories, and folk religious traditions.
A collection of West African folktales, including trickster and animal stories often associated with Anansi and oral storytelling traditions.
A medieval German heroic epic involving Siegfried, Kriemhild, dragons, treasure, betrayal, and mythic heroic tradition.
An ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, set during the Trojan War and focusing on the quarrel between King Agamemnon and the warrior Achilles.
A collection of Old Norse mythological and heroic poems, preserving major stories about Odin, Thor, Loki, Ragnarok, and legendary heroes.

id: Kalevala The Epic Poem of Finland
description: The Finnish national epic, compiled from oral poetry, featuring creation myths, magic songs, heroes, shamans, and mythic quests.
A collection of medieval Welsh tales containing Celtic mythological motifs, heroic legends, magical transformations, and Arthurian material.
A collection of Aboriginal Australian legends and Dreaming-related stories recorded from oral traditions.
A collection of ancient legends and traditions of the Maori, including mythic figures, ancestral stories, ritual specialists, and sacred knowledge.
An English verse translation of the Ramayana, one of India's great mythological epics, narrating the deeds of Rama, Sita, Hanuman, and Ravana.
The mythic and heroic sagas of the K'iche' Maya, including creation narratives, the Hero Twins, divine ancestry, and sacred history.
An English translation of the Qur'an, the central sacred text of Islam, presenting revelations associated with the Prophet Muhammad.
A central sacred scripture of Hinduism, presenting a philosophical dialogue between Prince Arjuna and the god Krishna on duty, honor, and the nature of reality.
The oldest surviving epic poem in Old English, telling the story of a Scandinavian hero who battles the monster Grendel, his vengeful mother, and a dragon.

id: The King James Version of the Bible
description: The canonical King James English translation of the Bible, central to Christian scripture and influential in English-language religious literature.

id: Legends of Ma-ui a Demi God of Polynesia
description: A collection of Polynesian legends about Maui, the trickster and culture hero known for feats such as fishing up islands and slowing the sun.
An ancient Greek epic poem attributed to Homer, recounting the long, mythic journey of the hero Odysseus returning to Ithaca after the fall of Troy.
An ancient Roman epic by Virgil that follows Aeneas, a Trojan hero who travels to Italy after the fall of Troy and becomes the ancestor of the Romans.
A collection of myths about the Dreamtime and traditional beliefs of the indigenous peoples of Australia.
A massive ancient Indian epic that narrates the Kurukshetra War between the Pandava and Kaurava princes, alongside vast philosophical and religious treatises.
One of the oldest deciphered writings of significant length in the world, providing a comprehensive Babylonian law code from ancient Mesopotamia.
